THE EF2 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST SW OF MOUNDS ILLINOIS AND MOVED ENE THROUGH MOUNDS AND CROSSED I-57 NEAR EXIT 8. THE TORNADO CROSSED THE OHIO RIVER NEAR THE TOWN OF AMERICA. IT MOVED ACROSS N. BALLARD AND MCCRACKEN COUNTIES IN KENTUCKY...IMPACTING OSCAR...BANDANA AND RAGLAND. IT CROSSED THE RIVER AGAIN AND LIFTED 2 MILES NW OF METROPOLIS ILLINOIS. PEAK WIND 120 MPH...AVERAGE PATH WIDTH WAS 125 YARDS AND THE PATH LENGTH 26.5 MILES.

THE FOLLOWING IS THE PRELIMINARY DAMAGE ASSESSMENT FOR JACKSON COUNTY ILLINOIS. * EVENT TYPE.........EF1 TORNADO * EVENT DATE.........FRIDAY MARCH 23 2012 * EVENT TIME.........BEGAN AT APPROXIMATELY 1224 PM CDT * EVENT LOCATION.....THE TORNADO BEGAN 3.5 NORTHWEST OF DESOTO ALONG TRUAX TRAER ROAD AND TRAVELED NORTHEAST TO 3.5 MILES SOUTHEAST OF DUQUOIN NEAR THE JACKSON...PERRY AND FRANKLIN COUNTY LINES. * PEAK WIND..........90 MPH * AVG. PATH WIDTH....125 YARDS * PATH LENGTH........7.5 MILES (THERE ARE SOME INDICATIONS THAT THE TORNADO WAS PERIODICALLY SKIPPING ALONG THE PATH) * INJURIES...........NONE * FATALITIES.........NONE * DAMAGE DETAIL......DOZENS OF TREES UPROOTED AND SNAPPED...MOST OF THE TREES WERE SMALL TO MEDIUM SIZED. ONE BARN DESTROYED. SEVERAL SHEDS AND CARPORTS DAMAGED. A FEW HOMES WITH MINOR DAMAGE TO SIDING. FLAG POLE BLOWN DOWN. THERE WERE NUMEROUS EYE WITNESSES AND NWS TRAINED SPOTTERS WHO SAW THIS TORNADO AND TOOK PICTURES AND/OR VIDEO.

DOWNBURST FROM 2.5 MILES NORTH OF DIEHLSTADT TO 1 MILE SOUTH OF DIEHLSTADT. PEAK WIND 100 MPH. PATH LENGTH 3.5 MILES. PATH WIDTH 1/2 MILE. 52 HOMES DAMAGED WITH MOSTLY MINOR TO MODERATE ROOF DAMAGE. SEVERAL BUSINESSES AND A CHURCH DAMAGED. NUMEROUS OUTBUILDINGS AND OTHER BUILDINGS DAMAGED. SEVERAL POWER POLES SNAPPED. DOZENS OF TREES UPROOTED OR SNAPPED. IRRIGATION SYSTEM TOPPLED.

*** 3 FATAL *** EVENT UPDATED FROM TSTM WND DMG TO EF-2 TORNADO. PATH 1/4 MILE NORTH OF DIEHLSTADT TO 1/4 MILE SOUTH OF DIEHLSTADT. PEAK WIND 115 MPH. PATH LENGTH 1/2 MILE. PATH WIDTH 75 YARDS. SINGLE WIDE MOBILE HOME DEMOLISHED WITH UNDERCARRIAGE BLOWN 100 YARDS. SEVERAL LARGE WAREHOUSE BUILDINGS WITH PARTIAL ROOF AND STRUCTURAL DAMAGE. SEVERAL TREES SNAPPED OR UPROOTED.

*** 3 FATAL *** UPDATED AND CORRECTED...TIME ADJUSTED BASED ON DOPPLER RADAR DATA. MISSOURI STATE POLICECONFIRMED 3 FATALITIES WITH NO INFORMATION CONCERNING INJURIES. STATE POLICE RECEIVED FIRST CALL AT 2109 PM LOCAL. LARGE HAIL WAS REPORTED WITH THIS STORM. NWS WILL PERFORM STORM DAMAGE SURVEY EARLY TUESDAY MORNING.
